title: cephaelis evea
intro: Cephaelis evea, also known as the West Indian jasmine or Jamaica quinine, is a plant species in the genus Cephaelis, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, malaria, and other infectious diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Cephaelis evea include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and lower body temperature, making it a valuable remedy for treating various health conditions. The herbal preparations of Cephaelis evea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from fever and pain to digestive issues and skin conditions.
